title: Kidambi Srikanth Reaches US Open Final, Showcases Sportsmanship
indian_angle: Indian shuttler Kidambi Srikanth's journey to the US Open final is a significant achievement for Indian badminton.
summary: Kidambi Srikanth has advanced to the US Open final, with a notable moment where he overturned a point given to him, demonstrating exceptional sportsmanship.

title: Rising Star Rounak Chouhan Stuns World No. 6 at US Open
indian_angle: Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan's victory over World No. 6 Chou Tien Chen marks a significant upset and highlights his potential.
summary: Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan, a self-proclaimed Cristiano Ronaldo fan, caused a major upset by defeating World No. 6 Chou Tien Chen at the US Open Super 300.

title: BAI Issues Addendum to Selection Criteria for Asia U17 & U15 Junior Championships
indian_angle: This addendum from BAI outlines crucial selection criteria for Indian junior players aiming for the Badminton Asia U17 & U15 Junior Championships.
summary: Badminton Association of India (BAI) has released a further addendum to the selection criteria for the upcoming Badminton Asia U17 & U15 Junior Championships 2026, detailing the requirements for aspiring young shuttlers.

title: Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Tournament Announced for Chennai in July
indian_angle: This circular announces a key domestic ranking tournament in Chennai, providing opportunities for Indian senior players to improve their national standings.
summary: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) has issued a circular for the Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament 2026, scheduled to take place in Chennai, Tamil Nadu, from July 16th to 23rd, 2026.

title: Thomas Cup Core Squad Reunites for Asian Games, Treesa-Gayatri Return
indian_angle: The core of India's Thomas Cup winning squad, including the return of Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand, is reassembling for the prestigious Asian Games.
summary: India's badminton squad for the Asian Games sees the return of key players, including the doubles pair Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand, as the Thomas Cup winning team reassembles for the continental multi-sport event.

title: India Submits Expression of Interest to Host BWF World Championships 2026
indian_angle: India's bid to host the BWF World Championships 2026 signifies a major step for the country's presence on the global badminton stage.
summary: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) has submitted an Expression of Interest (EOI) to host the BWF World Championships in 2026, indicating a strong desire to bring the premier global event to India.
